summary: The idea is sound. Store reports as data, not standalone HTML, and lock the schema before building. The viewer stays a lean shell and each report is a JSON file in its agent's folder, so the source is small, MCP-editable, and never hits the write cap.

lenses.name: Red
lenses.body: A self-contained file with inlined data blows past ~30KB and risks the silent clip that corrupted file-chunker. Fix: separate data from viewer so the HTML stays small and commits clean.

lenses.name: Professionalism
lenses.body: Home copy was stale and the report view leaned on nested cards (messy on mobile). Fixed: refreshed home, reports render as a flat document surface.

lenses.name: Development
lenses.body: Lock a report JSON schema (id, type, ts, target, verdict, summary, tally, plus findings/apps/clean for audits and lenses/call for reviews). One shape, every agent emits it.

call: Ship the data-separated build: lean viewer in agent-reports/, reports as per-agent JSON under brain-config/agents/<agent>/reports/. Source stays small and editable, every future report is just a committed JSON file.
